title: Cargo workspace + error types + logging scaffold
description:
Project scaffolding that every other slice-1 bead lands on top of.

Layout:
- single crate `hivemind` (workspace if multi-crate later)
- src/ledger/, src/projector/, src/commands/, src/queries/, src/cli/
- Cargo.toml with: rusqlite (or sqlx), kuzu, serde, serde_json, thiserror, tracing, tracing-subscriber, clap

Error type:
- thiserror-based domain errors: LedgerError, ProjectorError, CommandError, QueryError, CliError
- crate-level Result<T, HivemindError> alias

Logging:
- tracing crate; subscriber initialized in main; structured logs

AC:
- cargo build clean
- cargo test passes (no real tests yet, just smoke)
- tracing emits at least one log line at startup
- Error types compile; conversions work

title: Event envelope + JSON schemas (7 event types)
description:
Define the Event envelope as a Rust struct + write JSON schemas for all 7 slice-1 event types.

Envelope:
- event_id (monotonic, server-assigned)
- event_uuid (client-generated)
- correlation_id, causation_event_id (optional)
- type (enum), actor_id, payload (typed JSON), ts

Event types:
- decision.proposed, decision.accepted, decision.rejected, decision.superseded
- evidence.recorded, hypothesis.recorded, relation.added

Schemas live in schemas/v0/<type>.json.

AC:
- Rust Event struct + per-payload enums
- 7 JSON schemas committed under schemas/v0/
- validate(event) -> Result function exists
- Unit tests: each fixture validates; obvious invalid payloads rejected

title: EventLedger trait + in-memory impl
description:
Define EventLedger trait. Provide in-memory impl for tests.

Trait surface:
- append(event) -> Result<EventId>  (assigns monotonic id; dedup on event_uuid is a no-op return)
- read(offset, limit) -> Vec<Event>
- replay_from(offset, callback)
- latest_offset() -> EventId

In-memory impl: Vec<Event> + HashSet<Uuid> for dedup.

AC:
- Trait compiles; in-memory impl passes a shared test suite
- Monotonic event_id across appends
- Duplicate event_uuid append returns the existing event_id (no double-insert)
- replay_from(0) yields events in order

description:

Event envelope: event_id (monotonic), event_uuid (client-generated for idempotency), correlation_id, causation_event_id, type, actor_id, payload (typed JSON), ts.

title: GraphView trait + Kuzu schema DDL
description:
Define GraphView trait. Set up Kuzu schema for 5 node types + 10 relation types.

Trait surface:
- upsert_node(NodeKind, id, properties)
- upsert_edge(RelationKind, from_id, to_id, properties)
- query(cypher, params) -> Vec<Row>
- wipe() (for rebuild)

Kuzu DDL:
- Node tables: Decision, Actor, Evidence, Option, Hypothesis
  - Decision.topic_keys is a STRING[] (denormalized; Topic is NOT a node)
- Relation tables: PROPOSED_BY, ACCEPTED_BY, REJECTED_BY, SUPERSEDES, BASED_ON, HAS_OPTION, CHOSE, ASSUMES, SUPPORTS, REFUTES

AC:
- DDL applies cleanly to an empty Kuzu DB
- Trait + Kuzu impl compiles
- All 10 relation tables created; topic_keys is queryable as an array
- File created at ./hivemind/graph.kuzu

title: Event-to-graph projection logic
description:
Consume EventLedger events; project each of 7 event types to graph mutations via GraphView.

Mapping:
- decision.proposed -> upsert Decision, PROPOSED_BY (Decision -> Actor), edges from payload refs (HAS_OPTION, CHOSE, ASSUMES, BASED_ON)
- decision.accepted -> ACCEPTED_BY
- decision.rejected -> REJECTED_BY
- decision.superseded -> SUPERSEDES (new -> old)
- evidence.recorded -> upsert Evidence
- hypothesis.recorded -> upsert Hypothesis
- relation.added -> generic edge upsert (SUPPORTS/REFUTES/etc.)

Full rebuild API: wipe + replay_from(0).

AC:
- All 7 event types project to correct nodes/edges
- Replay-from-zero produces identical graph state across runs
- Rebuild of 10k events under 1s
- Stays in conservative Cypher (no Kuzu-only extensions)

title: Kuzu projector + GraphView trait
description:
Projector consuming the EventLedger and building a Kuzu graph projection. Implements the GraphView trait so future Neo4j backend slots in.

Builds 5 node types: Decision, Actor, Evidence, Option, Hypothesis.
Builds 10 relation types: PROPOSED_BY, ACCEPTED_BY, REJECTED_BY, SUPERSEDES, BASED_ON, HAS_OPTION, CHOSE, ASSUMES, SUPPORTS, REFUTES.

Topic is a string property on Decision (topic_keys: [string]); not a node.

AC:
- consumes all 7 slice-1 event types and produces correct nodes/edges
- idempotent (replay produces identical graph state)
- full rebuild from ledger
- on-disk location: ./hivemind/graph.kuzu
- Cypher stays conservative (no Kuzu-specific extensions) so the Neo4j swap stays cheap
- GraphView trait sufficient for slice-1 queries; engine-specific code isolated to one adapter

title: Entity-creation commands + topic normalization
description:
First slice of the commands module: entities that exist on their own. Plus topic_keys normalization helper.

Commands:
- record_evidence(actor, content) -> EvidenceId  -> emits evidence.recorded
- record_hypothesis(actor, statement) -> HypothesisId  -> emits hypothesis.recorded
- record_option(actor, label, description) -> OptionId  (no event; Option is a child entity in slice 1, attached via relation.added during propose_decision)

Topic normalization:
- normalize_topic_key(s) -> String  (lowercase, slugify, strip punctuation)
- Property tests for idempotence + ASCII slug correctness

AC:
- 3 commands implemented; each writes to ledger correctly
- Topic normalization unit-tested for unicode, whitespace, punctuation, length cap
- Invariant: actor_id present

title: Decision-lifecycle commands
description:
Core decision lifecycle.

Commands:
- propose_decision(actor, title, rationale, topic_keys, option_ids, hypothesis_ids, evidence_ids) -> DecisionId
  -> emits decision.proposed (with refs in payload) + relation.added events for HAS_OPTION, CHOSE (if specified), ASSUMES, BASED_ON
- accept_decision(decision_id, actor) -> emits decision.accepted
- reject_decision(decision_id, actor) -> emits decision.rejected
- supersede_decision(old_id, new_id, actor) -> emits decision.superseded

Invariants:
- All referenced ids must exist in the ledger before linking
- Same actor cannot both accept and reject the same decision (reject at write)
- supersede: both decisions must exist; the old one need not be accepted
- topic_keys normalized via normalize_topic_key (from B3.1)

AC:
- 4 commands implemented
- Per-invariant unit tests (positive + negative)
- propose_decision correctly fans out into multiple events with proper causation_event_id linkage

title: Relation commands
description:
After-the-fact relation attachment.

Commands:
- attach_evidence(decision_id, evidence_id, actor) -> emits relation.added (Decision BASED_ON Evidence)
- relate_evidence_to_hypothesis(evidence_id, hypothesis_id, kind: supports|refutes, actor) -> emits relation.added (Evidence SUPPORTS/REFUTES Hypothesis)

Invariants:
- Both endpoints must exist
- kind must be supports OR refutes
- Same (evidence, hypothesis, kind) tuple is idempotent (don't double-emit)

AC:
- 2 commands implemented
- Idempotency tested
- Invariant unit tests

title: Commands module (invariant enforcement layer)
description:
The only place rules and invariants live. CLI, future HTTP, future MCP all go through this. Pure Rust functions, no service overhead.

Commands:
- propose_decision(actor, title, rationale, topic_keys, option_ids, hypothesis_ids, evidence_ids) -> Decision
- accept_decision(decision_id, actor)
- reject_decision(decision_id, actor)
- supersede_decision(old_id, new_id, actor)
- attach_evidence(decision_id, evidence_id, actor)
- record_evidence(actor, content) -> Evidence
- record_hypothesis(actor, statement) -> Hypothesis
- record_option(actor, label, description) -> Option
- relate_evidence_to_hypothesis(evidence_id, hypothesis_id, kind, actor)

Invariants:
- All referenced ids must exist before linking
- supersede: both decisions exist; old need not be accepted
- Same actor cannot both accept and reject the same decision (different actors disagreeing IS allowed -> contested)
- topic_keys normalized (lowercase, slug) before persistence

AC:
- 9 commands implemented
- Unit tests cover every invariant rule
- Topic normalization tested with edge cases
- Each command produces the right event(s) and calls EventLedger.append

title: Status derivation (Decision + Hypothesis)
description:
Pure functions computing derived status from graph edges. NO storage of status as a property.

Decision status (5 cases):
- superseded: incoming SUPERSEDES from another Decision
- contested: ACCEPTED_BY AND REJECTED_BY both exist
- accepted: ACCEPTED_BY exists, REJECTED_BY does not
- rejected: REJECTED_BY exists, ACCEPTED_BY does not
- proposed: only PROPOSED_BY (none of the above)

Hypothesis status:
- refuted: >=1 REFUTES
- supported: >=1 SUPPORTS and 0 REFUTES
- open: neither

AC:
- Pure functions: derive_decision_status(graph, id), derive_hypothesis_status(graph, id)
- All 5 Decision cases covered by unit tests
- Hypothesis 3 cases covered
- No graph mutations; idempotent on call

title: Queries: get_decision + get_relevant_decisions
description:
Two basic read queries.

- get_decision(id) -> Decision + immediate neighbors (chosen Option, all HAS_OPTION Options, BASED_ON Evidence, ASSUMES Hypothesis with its derived status) + derived Decision status
- get_relevant_decisions(topic, status?) -> all Decisions whose topic_keys contains the normalized topic; optional filter by derived status

Response envelope:
{ result_count, truncated, latency_ms, data }

AC:
- Both queries implemented
- Hard ceiling at 1000 nodes per response
- Topic filter uses Kuzu array contains on topic_keys
- Integration tests against a small in-memory fixture
- Status filter works for all 5 Decision states including contested

title: Query: get_supersession_chain + cycle-guard
description:
Walk SUPERSEDES edges both directions from a Decision.

- get_supersession_chain(decision_id) -> ordered list of Decisions (older -> newer) with the input position marked
- Cycle-guard: if a cycle is detected, returns a structured CycleDetected error with the offending edge; does not loop

AC:
- Walks both ancestor and descendant direction
- Detects fabricated cycles in test fixtures (returns error, no infinite loop)
- Returns deterministic order
- Empty chain (no SUPERSEDES) returns just the input decision

title: Queries module (3 read ops, layer 2)
description:
Pure reads over the Kuzu projection. No LLMs, no ranking, no inference beyond status derivation.

Queries:
- get_decision(id) -> Decision + immediate neighbors (options, chosen option, evidence, hypotheses) + derived status
- get_relevant_decisions(topic, status?) -> all decisions with that topic_key; optionally filtered by derived status
- get_supersession_chain(decision_id) -> walk SUPERSEDES both directions; return ordered list with cycle-guard

Status derivation (computed at query time from edges, not stored):
- superseded: incoming SUPERSEDES from another Decision
- contested: both ACCEPTED_BY and REJECTED_BY edges exist
- accepted: ACCEPTED_BY exists, REJECTED_BY does not
- rejected: REJECTED_BY exists, ACCEPTED_BY does not
- proposed: only PROPOSED_BY so far

Hypothesis status (open/supported/refuted) derived similarly from SUPPORTS/REFUTES counts.

AC:
- All 3 queries implemented
- Derived status correct for all 5 Decision cases including contested
- Supersession chain walks both directions; cycle-guard returns structured error
- Hard ceiling at 1000 nodes per response
- Each response includes result_count, truncated (false in slice 1), latency_ms
- Integration tests against fixtures

title: CLI scaffolding (clap, --actor, JSON output)
description:
Top-level binary scaffolding using clap.

Top-level flags:
- --actor <id>  (defaults to a configured user id)
- --json        (machine-readable output for query subcommand)
- --hivemind-dir <path>  (default: ./hivemind/)
- -v / --verbose (tracing level)

Error reporting:
- non-zero exit on command/query failure
- structured errors mapped to typed exit codes (1 = generic, 2 = validation, 3 = invariant, 4 = storage)

AC:
- cargo run -- --help renders top-level help
- --actor flag plumbed through to commands
- Subcommands (emit/query/dump) registered but stubs OK at this bead's boundary
- Tracing level configurable via -v / -vv

title: CLI: 'hivemind emit' subcommand
description:
Wraps the 9 commands of B3.1-B3.3.

Forms (examples):
- hivemind emit decision.proposed --title "..." --rationale "..." --topic-keys foo,bar --options opt1,opt2 --chose opt1 --hypotheses h1 --evidence e1
- hivemind emit decision.accepted --decision-id <id>
- hivemind emit decision.superseded --old <id> --new <id>
- hivemind emit evidence.recorded --content "..."
- hivemind emit hypothesis.recorded --statement "..."
- hivemind emit relation.added --kind supports --from <evidence_id> --to <hypothesis_id>

AC:
- All 9 commands callable from CLI
- Returns the new entity id (or event id) on stdout
- Bad input produces a structured error (exit code 2)
- --json mode emits structured JSON instead of free text

title: CLI: 'hivemind query' subcommand
description:
Wraps the 3 queries of B4.2-B4.3.

Forms:
- hivemind query get_decision --id <id>
- hivemind query get_relevant_decisions --topic <topic> [--status <s>]
- hivemind query get_supersession_chain --id <id>

JSON output mandatory (the query layer's natural output format).

AC:
- All 3 queries callable from CLI
- Output is valid JSON conforming to the response envelope (result_count, truncated, latency_ms, data)
- Empty result sets return well-formed JSON, not errors
- --status filter accepts all 5 Decision states including contested

title: CLI: 'hivemind dump --format dot'
description:
Whole-graph DOT export for human inspection.

- Reads all nodes + edges from the current Kuzu projection
- Emits Graphviz DOT to stdout
- Colors/labels per node type so the graph is readable

AC:
- hivemind dump --format dot > graph.dot writes a parseable DOT file
- graphviz dot -Tpng renders it without errors
- Decision nodes show title and derived status; Hypothesis nodes show status
- DOT is deterministic ordering (so diffs are reviewable)

title: CLI: hivemind emit | query | dump
description:
Thin CLI wrapper. emit -> commands module; query -> queries module; dump -> DOT export of current graph state.

Subcommands:
- hivemind emit <command> [--actor <id>] [args...]   (wraps commands)
- hivemind query <op> [args...]                       (wraps queries; JSON output)
- hivemind dump --format dot > graph.dot              (whole projection as Graphviz DOT)

Out of scope for slice 1: init, rebuild, replay, log, snapshot (defer to slice 2 if useful).

AC:
- All 3 subcommands work end-to-end against real SQLite + Kuzu
- DOT output renders cleanly with graphviz
- JSON output for query subcommand is valid JSON
- --actor flag works; defaults to a configured user id

title: Seed dataset generator (deterministic)
description:
Reproducible seed of >=30 fabricated decisions covering slice-1 demo cases.

Coverage:
- >=1 superseded decision chain (length >=3)
- >=1 contested decision (both ACCEPTED_BY and REJECTED_BY from different actors)
- >=1 Hypothesis with >=1 SUPPORTS, then later >=1 REFUTES (status flips to refuted), invalidating >=2 decisions that ASSUMES it
- >=3 distinct topics
- >=1 decision with multiple options + a CHOSE'n one

Implementation:
- A bash or Rust harness that emits the events through hivemind emit (or via the commands module directly)
- Deterministic: same script run twice produces identical event sequence

AC:
- Script committed to repo (e.g., tests/seed/seed.sh or tests/seed/seed.rs)
- Documented command: 'cargo test --test seed -- --include-ignored' (or equivalent) populates ./hivemind/
- Re-running on a clean state yields byte-identical ledger

title: Golden snapshot harness + 3 query snapshots
description:
Integration test harness that runs all 3 queries against the seed dataset and diffs against committed golden JSON.

Includes:
- Test that wipes ./hivemind/, runs the seed generator, runs the 3 query subcommands, captures JSON outputs
- Diffs against snapshots/golden/<query>.json
- Regen command documented: 'cargo test golden -- --bless' or similar

AC:
- 3 golden JSON files committed (one per query op)
- Integration test green on clean checkout
- Failing diff prints unified diff for review
- Regen flag documented in CONTRIBUTING.md (or README)

title: Seed dataset + integration tests
description:
Fabricated seed dataset covering the slice-1 demo cases, plus golden-snapshot integration tests.

Coverage:
- >=30 fabricated decisions
- >=1 superseded chain
- >=1 contested decision (both ACCEPTED_BY and REJECTED_BY)
- >=1 hypothesis flipped to refuted that invalidates >=2 decisions ASSUMES it
- >=3 distinct topics
- >=1 decision with multiple options + a chosen one

Golden snapshots for each of the 3 queries against the seed data.

AC:
- Seed dataset is reproducible (deterministic from a fixed sequence of CLI emits)
- Golden JSON snapshots committed to the repo
- Integration test re-runs the queries and diffs against goldens; PR fails on diff
- Snapshots regenerable via a documented command

title: Replay smoke test (warning-only)
description:
Smoke test (not a release gate per slice-1 design).

Procedure:
- Run B6.1 seed against a fresh ./hivemind/
- Capture all 3 query JSON outputs (A)
- Delete ./hivemind/graph.kuzu (NOT the ledger)
- Re-project from ledger
- Re-run the 3 queries (B)
- Diff A vs B

AC:
- Test runs in under 5s on the seed dataset
- Diverging output prints a per-query unified diff but exits 0 with a warning log
- CI is configured to surface the warning but NOT fail on it (per user direction: 'smoke test, not gate')
- Non-zero exit only on infrastructure error (e.g., Kuzu won't open)
- README mentions the test and its non-gate status

title: Replay smoke test (warning, not gate)
description:
Wipe Kuzu, re-project from the SQLite ledger, re-run all 3 queries, and confirm byte-identical JSON output.

In CI: runs on every build but reports as a warning if it diverges, not a failure. Slice 1 design choice (per user direction: 'why replay?' -> 'smoke test, not gate'). Useful for debugging projector bugs; gating waits until we have a real projector to break.

AC:
- Test exists, runs in <5s on the seed dataset
- Diverging output emits a clear warning with a per-query diff
- Documented exit-code semantics: 0 on match, 0 with warning on diff, non-zero only on infrastructure error
- README mentions the smoke test and that it is not a release gate
